health_zone_table.basis: SitRep55 Table 2 (2026-07-08) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP PDF (post 25123, media 25124, pdf sha256 44706aea5157df826e05f4be706abca3acf93033d0e586a31c8e03711a94d91b, 12 pages, 1204669 bytes), byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt.

reconciliation.note: Named health-zone rows sum to 1772 confirmed / 624 confirmed deaths. Table 1 province totals are Ituri 1631/535, Nord-Kivu 158/89, Sud-Kivu 3/1 = national 1792/625. The Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified is retained as the explicit unventilated row. Nord-Kivu's Table 1 total (158/89) exceeds the sum of its Table 2 named zone rows (155/88) by 3 confirmed / 1 death; those 3 cases and 1 death are reported at province level but not yet ventilated to a named Nord-Kivu zone this cycle, so the derived unallocated residual is 20 confirmed / 1 death (17 Ituri + 3 Nord-Kivu). The source challenge table cites only the 17 Ituri unventilated cases; the additional Nord-Kivu 3/1 is an unacknowledged Table 1 vs Table 2 gap, disclosed here and never smeared across named zones.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 157 analyzed / 30 positive (19.1%); Nord-Kivu 113 analyzed / 3 positive (2.7%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 33 positives over 270 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep55 Tableau 7: 764 in isolation at end of day (236 confirmed / 528 suspected) at 95.3% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu at 133.0% saturation, Ituri 87.0%, Sud-Kivu 60%. Exits over 24h: 10 recoveries (all Ituri), 25 deaths (suspect/confirmed, all Ituri), 95 non-cases, 7 escapes (all Ituri), 1 transfer to HGR; 151 admissions across the three provinces.
Recovered advanced to 295 DRC cumulative (+10). J-1 bed count is 751; the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 764 (236 + 528).
highlights: 33 new confirmed cases and 25 deaths (44% community) were reported for 2026-07-08, in Ituri (30 cases, 24 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (3 cases, 1 death). Confirmed cases rose to 1792 and deaths to 625; the banner CFR is 34.9%. The headline is flagged 'Donnees reactualisees' (DHIS2 base cleaning/harmonization).

No newly affected health zone this cycle; affected zones hold at 37/104. Two cases were detected in Kisangani (Tshopo), one linked to the Nia-Nia health zone; investigations are ongoing and the three officially affected provinces remain unchanged, so the cases are EXCLUDED from totals and carried on the suspected-watch overlay.
The provider strike continues in Bunia and Rwampara (Ituri). Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 133% and global bed occupancy is 95.3% (764 in isolation).

Ituri concentrates 91.0% of cases (n=1631) and 85.6% of deaths (n=535), CFR 32.8%. Bunia (487), Rwampara (370), Mongbwalu (315), Nyankunde (97) and Nizi (104) remain the most affected zones and hold ~84.2% of provincial cases; Nizi is progressing faster than other zones.
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial CFR at 56.3%, led by Katwa (67.2%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (65.5%). Sud-Kivu remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death (no new case since 2026-05-26).
laboratory: SitRep55 reports Ituri collected/analyzed 157 samples with 30 positive (19.1%).
Nord-Kivu collected/analyzed 113 samples with 3 positive (2.7%). Sud-Kivu collected 2 samples under analysis. National: 33 positive over 270 analyzed (12.2%).
publication_context: INSP SitRep 55 reports through 2026-07-08 (post 25123; WP publish 2026-07-09T21:03:28Z; PDF media 25124). The report date is 08 July 2026; publication date 09 July 2026.
SitRep55 is the current/latest operational snapshot; the cumulative rose by 33 confirmed and 25 deaths over 2026-07-08 vs SitRep54.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 37 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone this cycle.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1631 of 1792 DRC confirmed cases (91.0%) and 535 deaths (CFR 32.8%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 898 alerts of the day were reported (59 J-1 + 841 new) and 605 investigated (67.4%); Ituri 54.1% (319/590), Nord-Kivu 92.5% (271/293), Sud-Kivu 100.0% (17/17). One narrative line misprints the investigated total as '898 (67.4%)'; the table value is 605.
271 suspected cases of the day were validated per Tableau 3 (211 living, 60 community deaths); the banner and narrative instead say 227. Ituri 202, Nord-Kivu 21, Sud-Kivu 4.

11927 contacts were under follow-up and 9376 seen (78.6%); Ituri 76.1% (9767 under / 7434 seen), Nord-Kivu 89.9% (2160 under / 1942 seen). 498 new contacts listed (Ituri 349, Nord-Kivu 149); 3 became suspect (all Nord-Kivu); 148 completed 21-day follow-up (Nord-Kivu 87, Ituri 61).

At PoE/PoC, 0 dead bodies were intercepted and 159912 persons passed through PoE/PoC this cycle (Ituri 103467, Nord-Kivu 56445), 94.9% screened. In Ituri, 7 live alerts were notified and validated at PoE/PoC, of which 6 were isolated and referred to CT/CTE.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 59; new alerts 841; total alerts of the day 898. Tableau 3 investigated 605 (67.4%). One narrative line misprints the investigated total as '898 (67.4%)'. The banner and narrative say 227 validated suspects while Tableau 3 totals 271 (211 living + 60 deaths); table values (898 / 605 / 271) are promoted, banner/narrative 227 retained as conflict.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 133.0%; national isolation occupancy is 95.3% (764 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 78.6% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting/investigation in Ituri (54.1% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers (112 HCW cases, 35 deaths, CFR 31.2%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.3% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two Kisangani (Tshopo) detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) are under investigation and 159912 travelers passed through PoE/PoC this cycle.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (08 July): 11927 under follow-up, 9376 seen, 78.6%. Ituri 76.1% (9767/7434), Nord-Kivu 89.9% (2160/1942). Tableau 5: 498 new contacts listed (Ituri 349, Nord-Kivu 149), 3 became suspect (all Nord-Kivu), 148 completed 21-day follow-up (Nord-Kivu 87, Ituri 61).

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep55 Tableau 7 end-of-day census 764 at 95.3% occupancy (Ituri 540 @87.0%, Nord-Kivu 197 @133.0%, Sud-Kivu 27 @60%). Census 764 = 236 confirmed + 528 suspected; province rows 540+197+27=764 concordant. J-1 bed count 751. Exits: 10 recoveries (all Ituri), 25 deaths (all Ituri), 95 non-cases, 7 escapes (all Ituri), 1 transfer to HGR (Nord-Kivu); 151 admissions; total exits 138.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; ventilate the 3 Nord-Kivu confirmed cases and 1 death that appear at province level but in no named Nord-Kivu zone; and validate the two Kisangani/Tshopo detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) before any count-bearing use
Relieve isolation load at 95.3% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 133% saturation (56.3% provincial CFR); close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction/expansion
Improve contact follow-up (78.6% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 54.1%, reinforce intervention teams in affected ZS, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog

preserved_conflicts: SitRep55 headline is DRC-only (1792 confirmed / 625 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.
CONFLICT (preserved): validated suspects of the day — Tableau 3 totals 271 (211 living + 60 deaths), while the banner and narrative say 227. The promoted daily-suspect count is 271.

CONFLICT (preserved): Nord-Kivu Table 1 total is 158 confirmed / 89 deaths, but the sum of its Table 2 named zone rows is 155 / 88; 3 confirmed cases and 1 death are reported at province level but not ventilated to a named Nord-Kivu zone this cycle. The source challenge table cites only the 17 Ituri unventilated cases. Promoted national is 1792 / 625; the derived unallocated residual is 20 confirmed / 1 death (17 Ituri + 3 Nord-Kivu), disclosed and not smeared across named zones.

CONFLICT (preserved): SitRep55 Tableau 3 alert totals are 898 alerts of the day and 605 investigated (67.4%); one narrative line misprints the investigated total as '898 (67.4%)'. The promoted values follow the table (898 alerts / 605 investigated / 67.4%).

SitRep55 Tableau 7 end-of-day isolation census is 764 (236 confirmed + 528 suspected; province rows Ituri 540 + Nord-Kivu 197 + Sud-Kivu 27 = 764) at 95.3% occupancy; the J-1 bed count is 751. The promoted census is 764.
SitRep55 supersedes SitRep54 (single report-day advance): +33 confirmed and +25 confirmed deaths for 2026-07-08. Ituri +30 (24 deaths), Nord-Kivu +3 (1 death), Sud-Kivu +0.
SitRep55 carries an Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone this cycle; the affected-zone count holds at 37 of 104 (same zone set as SitRep54).

Two cases were detected in Kisangani (Tshopo), one linked to the Nia-Nia health zone; investigations are ongoing and the three officially affected provinces remain unchanged. They are EXCLUDED from SitRep55 totals and carried on the existing suspected-watch overlay, not as counted cases (sourceConflict key sr55_kisangani_tshopo_suspected_watch).

The SitRep55 PDF is labeled 'Draft_...' but is attached to the official INSP SR55 post (25123, media 25124) and used as the official receipt unless INSP supersedes it. The banner flags 'Donnees reactualisees' (DHIS2 base cleaning/harmonization).

Uganda anchor carried at 20 cumulative confirmed / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (Uganda MoH, last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1812 confirmed / 627 deaths / 306 recovered. NOTE: the Uganda EVD dashboard (evd-daily.health.go.ug) on 2026-07-09 shows 17 recoveries; the recovered anchor is deliberately held at 11 for series consistency pending a formal re-anchor decision, matching SitRep54's treatment.

CONFLICT (preserved): the Nord-Kivu narrative names Katwa 'n=62' as the top-notifying zone, but Table 2 lists Katwa at 61 confirmed / 41 deaths (CFR 67.2% = 41/61); the table value 61 is authoritative and the narrative 'n=62' is treated as a typo.
Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death (Miti-Murhesa).
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1, Boga 1/1); unstable small-number ratios.

review_notes: SitRep #055 (report date 2026-07-08; INSP post 25123; media 25124; WP publish 2026-07-09T21:03:28Z). PDF sha256 44706aea5157df826e05f4be706abca3acf93033d0e586a31c8e03711a94d91b, 12 pages, 1204669 bytes, byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt.

SitRep55 supersedes SitRep54 (single report-day advance). DRC headline 1792 confirmed / 625 confirmed deaths (CFR 34.9%); +33 confirmed and +25 confirmed deaths vs SitRep54. On 08 July, 33 new confirmed (Ituri 30, Nord-Kivu 3) carried 44% community deaths.

No newly affected health zone this cycle; affected-zone count holds at 37 of 104 (same zone set as SitRep54). Named health-zone rows reconcile to 1772 confirmed / 624 deaths; with the 17 Ituri unventilated cases the derived unallocated residual is 20 confirmed / 1 death because Nord-Kivu's Table 1 total (158/89) exceeds its Table 2 named zone rows (155/88) by 3 cases / 1 death.
Two cases were detected in Kisangani (Tshopo), one linked to Nia-Nia, EXCLUDED from totals pending investigation; carried on the existing suspected-watch overlay, not counted.
Tableau 7 end-of-day census 764 (236 confirmed / 528 suspected) at 95.3% occupancy; Nord-Kivu 133.0% saturation. Recovered advanced to 295 DRC cumulative. J-1 bed count 751.
Laboratory: Ituri 157 analyzed / 30 positive (19.1%); Nord-Kivu 113 analyzed / 3 positive (2.7%); Sud-Kivu 2 under analysis (270 analyzed / 33 positive overall, 12.2%).

Uganda anchor carried at 20 / 2 / 11 (last reviewed 2026-06-21); country-scope 1812 / 627 / 306. The Uganda dashboard shows 17 recoveries; the anchor is held at 11 for series consistency pending a formal re-anchor. Provider strike continues in Bunia and Rwampara; funding gap ~USD 20M.
